rulings.Case Title: WELTON OLIVER JR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Its Expert "Pared-Down" List Pursuant To C.C.P. § 2034.720
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, January 28, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant CALAVERAS ASBESTOS, LTD.¿s motion for leave to file untimely expert ¿pared-down¿ list Pursuant To C.C.P. § 2034.720 is granted. Good cause exists for the relief sought. Plaintiff¿s request that Calaveras bear the cost of deposing Mr. Spielman is denied. Plaintiff would typically incur the cost of deposing this witness and Calaveras¿ failure to timely submit its ¿pared down¿ list was the result of mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect with no prejudice to plaintiff. Calaveras¿ request for monetary sanctions is denied. Plaintiff¿s ¿opposition¿ in the form of seeking an order that Calaveras bear the cost of deposing Mr. Spielman, while unsuccessful, did not lack ¿substantial justification. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH GOFORTH VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Re-Open Discovery To Take The Deposition Of Non-Party Robert Mingram
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, January 28, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. DEFENDANT MONTEREY MECHANICAL COMPANY's MOTION To Re-Open Discovery To Take The Deposition Of Non-Party Robert Mingram. Hearing required. The Court did not receive the opposition papers with sufficient time to prepare a tentative ruling. =(220/HEK)
